name: Grandpa B's Whole Wheat Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, thoroughly mix the whole wheat flour, oatmeal, cracked wheat, dry milk, salt, sugar, and undissolved yeast.
2. In a separate bowl, combine warm water, vegetable oil, and molasses.
3. Add the liquid mixture to the dry ingredients. Mix for 2 minutes at medium speed of an electric mixer, scraping the sides of the bowl occasionally.
4. Gradually add all-purpose flour (1/4 cup at a time) until a soft dough forms.
5. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured pastry cloth or board.
6. Knead the dough for about 10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
7. Place the dough in a greased bowl, turning to grease the top. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled in bulk, about 45 minutes to 1 hour.
8. Punch down the dough and let it rise again until doubled.
9. Punch down the dough and let it rest for 5 minutes.
10. Shape the dough into a loaf and place it in a greased 8 1/2 x 4 1/2 x 2 1/2 inch bread pan.
11. Cover and let the dough rise until doubled, then bake in a preheated oven at 350°F for 45-50 minutes.
This recipe works very well in automatic breadmakers. Follow the directions for your particular machine.
